title: Use robust cryptographic keys
description: Weak cryptographic keys compromise the security of encryption systems. Short key lengths, predictable key generation, or weak key materials make encrypted data vulnerable to brute force attacks and cryptanalysis.
recommendation: Use cryptographically strong keys with appropriate lengths and generate them using secure random sources.

noncompliant.description: Weak key sizes and predictable key generation compromise cryptographic security.
Using weak ciphers and non-cryptographic random sources creates vulnerabilities.

compliant.description: Use strong key sizes and cryptographically secure random generation.
Use strong ciphers like AES-256, secure curves like P-256, and proper key derivation functions.

checks.message: Use robust cryptographic keys with appropriate lengths and secure random generation
